growth_records.medium: bicarbonate-buffered freshwater medium (pH 7.0), reduced with sodium sulfide, amended with glucose and yeast extract

up.units: not reported as rate (10 mM supplied as sole electron/carbon source)
up.method: batch cultivation; consumption not quantified as a gDW-normalized rate

sec.units: ppmv (headspace volume fraction; NOT gDW-normalized, no biomass measured)

growth_records.cite: Dong Xiyang et al. Fermentative Spirochaetes mediate necromass recycling in anoxic hydrocarbon-contaminated habitats. The ISME Journal (2018). DOI:10.1038/s41396-018-0148-3. [PMC6052044]
growth_records.snippet: we performed pure culture growth experiments with 10 mM glucose as the electron and carbon source. Gas chromatography demonstrated that H2 rapidly accumulated in the headspace during cultivation with glucose, resulting in H2 concentrations
growth_records.notes: Qualitative physiology paper; NO gDW-normalized uptake/secretion rates, NO measured specific growth rate, and NO biomass (gDW/OD) reported for R. cohabitans, so qS and qP cannot be computed. Only headspace H2 accumulation (ppmv) was measured by GC. Medium: R. cohabitans HM grown in bicarbonate-buffered freshwater medium pH 7.0 + 0.5 mM Na2S + 10 mM glucose + 0.1% yeast extract, 30 C, stationary, anaerobic (Methods 'Bacterial cultures and cultivation'). Base freshwater medium salt composition is cited from reference [5] (Bergmann/Meckenstock) and is NOT reproduced verbatim in this article. A speculative doubling time of ~13 days (shortening to ~11 days with necromass recycling) is discussed in the Discussion, but that value is for Desulfobacterium N47 from a hypothetical exponential-growth argument (10% inoculum -> full density in 6 weeks), NOT a measured R. cohabitans rate, so it is not encoded as growth_rate/doubling_time here. Ethanol and acetate are genome-predicted endproducts, not quantified. No isotopic/13C-MFA performed.

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 140272 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/140272
